genericMotivation:

companyValuesAnswer: I resonate most with values centered on customer focus, ownership, and building with integrity. In my work at Deutsche Telekom Digital Labs and Alteryx, I have seen that the best engineering outcomes come from taking responsibility end-to-end, collaborating openly, and building systems that are maintainable and secure. I am motivated by teams that value curiosity, high standards, and practical impact.
opportunityGoalsAnswer: I am looking for a role where I can build reliable, scalable backend systems, take ownership of meaningful product features, and grow with a strong engineering team. I want to contribute quickly using my experience with Java, Spring Boot, microservices, and production operations while continuing to learn from experienced peers.
complexQueryAnswer: At Deutsche Telekom Digital Labs, one of the more complex queries I worked on involved joining customer profile, subscription, entitlement, and usage/event tables across services to support reporting and troubleshooting for API-backed features. The query spanned roughly 5-6 related tables/views, included filtering by time windows and tenant identifiers, and required careful indexing and pagination to keep latency acceptable under production load.
yesFollowUpDescription: At Deutsche Telekom Digital Labs, I have worked on database and API performance by analyzing slow queries, adding indexes, tuning pagination, and using application metrics and logs to identify bottlenecks in microservice endpoints. I also used load testing and monitoring during releases to validate latency and error-rate improvements.

companyMotivations.Anthropic:
Anthropic stands out to me because of its commitment to building AI that is not only powerful, but also reliable, interpretable, and aligned with human values. The company’s focus on AI safety and research, alongside shipping world-class products like Claude, shows a balance of innovation and responsibility that I find compelling.

As an engineer, I'm excited by the opportunity to work on technically challenging problems at the frontier of AI while contributing to systems that can have a positive long-term impact on society. I’m particularly drawn to Anthropic’s culture of rigorous thinking, continuous learning, and its mission to ensure that advanced AI remains beneficial and trustworthy.
companyMotivations.Block: Economic empowerment means giving people and businesses access to tools and opportunities that help them participate more fully in the economy, regardless of their background or circumstances. customAnswers.How has AI changed the way you approach: AI has become an important part of my workflow, helping me quickly understand codebases, generate initial solutions, write tests, and troubleshoot issues. customAnswers.Do you have experience in building agent:
I have experience building AI-powered applications and experimenting with agentic workflows, including systems that use LLMs to analyze information, make decisions, and perform multi-step actions. For example, I built a job application automation project that uses AI to parse job descriptions, evaluate fit against a candidate profile, generate tailored application content, and automate parts of the application workflow.

While I have not yet deployed a large-scale agentic system in a production environment serving external customers, I have hands-on experience with the core concepts involved, including tool calling, workflow orchestration, prompt engineering, and integrating LLMs into backend services. I am particularly interested in applying these skills to production-grade agentic systems and continuing to deepen my experience in this area.

customAnswers.Most modern windows use double pane glas: Assumptions: steady state, 1 m² area, 1 mm air gap, inner pane 20°C, outer 0°C, ignore solar gain. Conduction through stagnant air gap dominates at this small gap; convection in a thin enclosed gap is weak; radiation between panes is secondary but non-zero. Order-of-magnitude: conduction ~ few watts to tens of watts for 20°C ΔT across 1 mm air (k_air ~ 0.026 W/m·K → q ≈ k·A·ΔT/Δx ≈ 0.52 W); convection smaller in narrow gap; radiation smaller than conduction here.

customAnswers.How would you achieve the linear convolu: Zero-pad both sequences to length ≥ 119 (typically next power of two, e.g. 128), compute FFTs, multiply in frequency domain, inverse FFT, then take the valid linear convolution segment (first 119 samples).
